calendar.matters:
Petition For Modification Of Trust
Off Calendar May-18-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mitchell Edwards for (petitioner) Barbara Bunting.

The Court stated that this is a petition to modify a trust, that it is clearly a family trying to do the right thing, that the legal issue is even when all beneficiaries agree the court still needs to inquire if it is consistent with the purposes of the trust, that this trust is set up to be there for future generations, that the court is left with balancing if the reason for doing this is a sufficient reason for overcoming the purpose. Mr. Edwards cited 15403 stating that everyone consented, that the purpose of future generations are the people consenting and that if the court denies the petition it will fall on the children to support her and they are unable to do so. The Court stated that either way they end up doing so. Mr. Edwards stated that the beneficiary is 105 years old and fragile, that without the funds they will need to move her and that they do not have the funds to support her. The Court asked if it was correct that it is costing $15,000.00 more than her income to keep her in this facility. Mr. Edwards confirmed this and stated that the family believes that the balancing of interests benefits everyone, that if she has to move it will cause financial and emotional distress and that if the court thinks it might deny the petition due to lack of information they can provide additional declarations. The Court took the petition for modification of the trust under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
